3.happening: We are about to sort intervals by start ascending, breaking ties by larger end first.
3.why: After sorting, any interval that could cover another appears earlier, so a single left-to-right sweep can detect covered intervals.
3.invariant: Once sorted, every interval's start is at least the start of all intervals before it.

4.why: count will track how many intervals survive without being covered.
4.invariant: No interval has been kept yet.

5.happening: We set prev_end to 0, the largest right endpoint kept so far.
5.why: Each new interval is compared against the farthest right end we have already kept.
5.invariant: prev_end is 0 before any interval is processed.

6.happening: We move to interval i = {i} in sorted order.
6.why: We examine the intervals left to right, so any covering interval is always seen first.
6.invariant: Every interval before i has already been decided.

7.why: The start makes the interval explicit; sorting already used it to order the sweep.
7.invariant: start is the left endpoint of interval i.

8.why: The end decides whether this interval extends beyond everything kept so far.
8.invariant: end is the right endpoint of interval i.

9.happening: end ({end}) is greater than prev_end ({prev_end}) — this interval reaches farther right than anything kept, so it is NOT covered.
9.why: Because intervals are sorted by start, an interval is covered only if an earlier one already reaches at least as far right; a strictly larger end proves this interval is new.
9.invariant: Every kept interval has the largest end seen so far.

9.happening: end ({end}) is not greater than prev_end ({prev_end}) — an earlier interval already covers this one, so we skip it.
9.why: An earlier interval has start <= this start and end >= this end, which is exactly the definition of covering.
9.invariant: Covered intervals change neither count nor prev_end.

10.happening: We keep this interval, so count becomes {count + 1}.
10.why: It is not covered by any earlier interval.
10.invariant: count equals the number of intervals kept so far.

11.happening: We update prev_end to {end}, the new farthest right endpoint.
11.why: Future intervals must reach beyond this to avoid being covered.
11.invariant: prev_end is the maximum end among all kept intervals.

12.why: Every interval has been checked; count is the number that survived.
12.invariant: count equals the number of intervals not covered by any other.

init.happening: Sort the intervals and initialize the counter and prev_end.
init.why: Sorting lets a single sweep detect covered intervals; the counter and prev_end drive that sweep.
init.invariant: No interval has been processed yet.

loop.happening: Advance to the next interval in sorted order.
loop.why: One left-to-right pass is enough once intervals are sorted.
loop.invariant: Intervals before i are already decided.

update.happening: Read the interval's endpoints or record that it was kept.
update.why: Each step either exposes the current interval or extends the kept range.

check.happening: Compare the current interval's end against prev_end.
check.why: A strictly larger end means the interval is not covered.
check.invariant: Covered intervals are skipped without changing state.

return.invariant: count is the number of intervals not covered by any other.
